scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained hypothesis- and protocol-design oriented and did not fabricate empirical results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays within research planning scope and does not cross into clinical or diagnostic decision-making.

methodological_ground.detail: Methods, controls, and validation logic are framed as proposals to be tested rather than established facts.

code_usability.detail: The package focuses on design logic and narrative outputs rather than executable scientific code.

functional_suitability.note: The package fully covers the SOP standardization task from fragmented notes to validated, checklist-auditable protocol documents.

reliability.note: The no-fabrication rule and placeholder pattern make the package dependable for messy inputs.

performance_context.note: The documentation-only execution model requires no runtime overhead beyond the LLM call needed to process the raw experimental notes.

agent_usability.note: The skill is immediately understandable and backed by a strong before/after example.

human_usability.note: The transformation from raw notes to standardized SOP is easy to inspect.

security.note: Safety and waste notes are part of the contract, not an afterthought.

maintainability.note: The template-and-guide structure is clean and low-risk.

agent_specific.note: The package is highly deterministic and resistant to unsupported inference.

inputs.label: Fragmented wet-lab notes
inputs.note: The transformation stayed disciplined and did not invent values.

assertions.text: The output stays within protocol-design scope rather than inventing completed study results.
assertions.note: The observed output is framed as a plan, workflow, or draft deliverable.

assertions.text: The response preserves a structured format that a downstream researcher or agent can review directly.
assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: Missing experimental parameters are marked explicitly instead of being inferred.
assertions.note: The no-fabrication contract is preserved throughout the output.

assertions.text: The standardized protocol includes operational checkpoints such as safety or quality-control notes.
assertions.note: The output carries beyond pure step formatting into operability checks.

key_strengths: The package has a strong no-fabrication contract for missing experimental parameters.
The example transformation clearly demonstrates the expected protocol shape and review checkpoints.
Safety, waste, and quality-control fields are integrated into the standardization flow.

recommendations.title: Add one example from a molecular assay workflow
recommendations.problem: The current example is strong, but it represents only one wet-lab pattern.
recommendations.root_cause: Example diversity is narrower than the documented task scope.
recommendations.fix: Add a second worked example for PCR, staining, or cell-culture workflows to widen transfer confidence.
